suppliers.relationship: related party contract design and manufacturing coordination support
suppliers.context: We use Ablecom Technology, Inc. (“Ablecom”) and its affiliate Compuware Technology, Inc. (“Compuware”), both of which are related parties, for contract design and manufacturing coordination support. We work with Ablecom to optimize modular designs for our chassis and several other components.

suppliers.relationship: related party contract design and manufacturing coordination support
suppliers.context: We use Ablecom Technology, Inc. (“Ablecom”) and its affiliate Compuware Technology, Inc. (“Compuware”), both of which are related parties, for contract design and manufacturing coordination support.

We work closely with the leading microprocessor, graphics processing units (“GPU”), memory, disk/flash, and interconnect vendors and other hardware and software suppliers to coordinate our new products’ design with their product release schedules. Much of our research and development activity relates to the new product cycles of leading processor vendors. We work closely with NVIDIA, Intel and AMD, among others.
We work closely with NVIDIA, Intel and AMD, among others, to develop products that are compatible with the latest generation of industry-standard technologies under development.

At the time of Leadtek Investment, Leadtek was, and it continues to be, an authorized reseller for us.

single_source_dependencies.context: Ablecom manufactured approximately 95.4%, 93.6%, and 91.9% of the chassis purchased by us during fiscal years 2025, 2024, and 2023, respectively. Since Ablecom manufactures substantially all the chassis that we incorporate into its products, if Ablecom were to suddenly be unable to manufacture chassis for us, our business could suffer.

geographic_concentration.context: We manufacture the majority of our systems at our San Jose, California headquarters.
Global assembly, test and quality control of our servers are performed at our manufacturing facilities in San Jose, California, Taiwan, the Netherlands, and Malaysia.
Our European headquarters for manufacturing and service operations is located in Den Bosch, the Netherlands.
Additionally as part of our efforts to expand our capacities, we added a new facility in Malaysia during the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025.

capacity_constraints.context: Additionally, during fiscal year 2025, the computer server industry experienced global supply chain shortages, which resulted in a need to carry more inventory to fulfill demand from our customers and partners.

supply_chain_risks.context: We rely on a limited number of suppliers for certain components used to manufacture our products. Two of our suppliers accounted for a significant portion of our total purchases: 64.4% and 5.1% in fiscal year 2025.

In October 2022, U.S. export restrictions and export licensing requirements were imposed targeting China’s semiconductor and supercomputing industries. These restrictions impacted certain of our products, including products that contain the NVIDIA A100 and H100 integrated circuits, among others.
The year-over-year increase in cost of sales was primarily attributed to... a $86.5 million or 493.6% increase in tariff expense related to new trade policies enacted during the year.

geographic_revenue.context: Sales to customers located outside of the United States represented 40.6%... of net sales in fiscal years 2025. United States net sales were $13,052.6 million (59.4%).
Asia net sales were $5,494.1 million, representing 25.0% of total net sales.
Europe net sales were $2,727.0 million, representing 12.4% of total net sales.
For the year ended June 30, 2025, 59.4% and 10.9% of revenues were from the United States and Thailand, respectively.

purchase_obligations.context: As of June 30, 2025, these remaining non-cancelable commitments were $1.6 billion, including $148.9 million to related parties.
In June 2024, we executed a long-term data center space from a supplier... on top of the estimated over $292.0 million financial obligation we have to the supplier for the term of the lease.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: borrowings under term loan and revolving lines of credit
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 10% change in interest rates would not have a significant impact
market_risk_disclosures.context: We are exposed to changes in interest rates as a result of our borrowings under our term loan and revolving lines of credit. Based on the outstanding principal indebtedness of $112.5 million under our credit facilities as of June 30, 2025, we believe that a 10% change in interest rates would not have a significant impact on the results of operations.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations (Netherlands, Taiwan, Malaysia)
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 10% change in foreign currency exchange rates would not have a significant impact
market_risk_disclosures.context: The functional currency of our subsidiaries in the Netherlands, Taiwan and Malaysia is the U.S. dollar. However, certain loans and transactions in these entities are denominated in a currency other than the U.S. dollar... a 10% change in foreign currency exchange rates would not have a significant impact on the results of operations.

inventory_composition.context: Finished goods inventory was $3,465,352 thousand as of June 30, 2025.
Work in process inventory was $674,613 thousand as of June 30, 2025.
Purchased parts and raw materials inventory was $540,410 thousand as of June 30, 2025.
